steps.title: Identify what s(t) represents
steps.content: The problem states that $s(t) = 136(0.5)^{\frac{t}{29}}$ gives the approximate mass, in grams, that REMAINS $t$ years after the 136 gram sample starts to decay. The key word is remains — so $s(t)$ is the remaining mass, not the amount lost.

steps.title: Interpret s(58) = 34 using the definition
steps.content:
Since $s(t)$ represents the remaining mass, $s(58) = 34$ means: at time $t = 58$ years, the remaining mass is 34 grams.

Check each choice:
  • A: Approximately 34 grams of the sample remains 58 years after the sample starts to decay — remaining mass = 34g, time = 58 years ✓
  • B: mass decreased by approximately 34 grams — this would mean 136 - 34 = 102g remaining, not 34g ✗
  • C: describes 34 years (wrong time) ✗
  • D: 58 grams remains after 34 years (wrong values) ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
